objective: Eliminate performance degradation from heavy analytical queries on production database and provide centralized analytics store with single source of truth by building a comprehensive data pipeline to extract data from MySQL to BigQuery with full historical load followed by daily incremental syncs
business_context: Heavy analytical queries are degrading production database performance. Organization needs a centralized analytics store with single source of truth to separate analytical workloads from production systems while maintaining data consistency and availability.
functional_requirements: Build data pipeline from MySQL to BigQuery
Perform full historical load of employee data
Implement daily incremental syncs using updated_at watermark column
Use UPSERT logic for data synchronization
Direct all reads to read-replica to avoid production impact
Ensure data deduplication in target system
Implement row count reconciliation with zero tolerance
Validate primary key uniqueness and not null constraints
Perform foreign key referential integrity checks

data_requirements.data_types: Employee data with primary key, updated_at timestamp, and related foreign key relationships
data_requirements.volume: Full historical load plus daily incremental updates
data_requirements.frequency: Daily incremental sync by 06:00 AM UTC

non_functional.sla: Data available by 06:00 AM UTC daily
non_functional.performance: Zero production database performance impact through read-replica usage
non_functional.scalability: Support for full historical load and ongoing incremental updates

security.access_controls: Read-replica access only for production database reads

constraints.technical_limitations: Must use read-replica to avoid production database impact

acceptance_criteria: Row count in BigQuery equals source table count with 0% tolerance
Airflow DAG completes before 06:00 AM UTC daily
dbt uniqueness test passes with 0 failures on primary key
Connection string points only to replica host
COUNT(*) reconciliation between source and target with zero tolerance
Primary key (id) must be not null and unique in curated layer
Foreign key referential integrity checks for order_items.order_id and payments.order_id
expected_outputs: Clean, deduplicated employee data in BigQuery curated layer
Daily data refresh completed by 06:00 AM UTC
Data pipeline with full historical and incremental sync capabilities
raw_text:
Ticket: SCRUM-64
Summary: Create automated data pipeline for Verizon analytics platform
Status: To Do
Priority: High
Assignee: Unassigned
Description:
Project Name: Verizon analytics platform Requirement Type: New_Dvlp Stakeholder: John Objective Eliminate performance degradation from heavy analytical queries on production database and provide centralized analytics store with single source of truth Build a comprehensive data pipeline to extract data from verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore (Agentichub.employees) to BigQuery (verizon_data_dea.employees). The pipeline must perform a full historical load followed by daily incremental syncs using updated_at watermark column with UPSERT logic. All reads must be directed to read-replica to avoid production database performance impact. Expected Outcome Clean, deduplicated data available in BigQuery curated layer by 06:00 AM UTC daily with zero production database impact Connections and DB Details Source: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore (IP: 34.70.79.163:3306), Database: Agentichub, Table: employees. Target: BigQuery project verizon-data, dataset verizon_data_dea, table employees Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: Row count in BigQuery equals source table count with 0% tolerance Airflow DAG completes before 06:00 AM UTC daily dbt uniqueness test passes with 0 failures on primary key Connection string points only to replica host Validation Rules: COUNT(*) reconciliation between source and target with zero tolerance Primary key (id) must be not null and unique in curated layer Foreign key referential integrity checks for order_items.order_id and payments.order_id
